content.text: Bullets aren't free — and they have serial numbers.
Precision is the marksman and the ammunition artist: the tree that turns the credit economy into a fire-control system. Every trigger pull is a line item, and the right round is the build.

The guns live on Weapons. The ammunition economy — abstracted standard ammo and its resupply costs, individually tracked specialty rounds, handloads, bootlegs, and the launch round catalog — lives on Consumables & Ammo. This page is what Precision does with all of it.

content.stat_lean: Per-weapon — guns roll the stat their weapon lists (see Weapons); the tree adds no stat of its own. The real lean is the wallet.
content.theme: One perfect shot, or the perfect round for this one: the long-band theater and the priced trigger pull.
content.design: No stat ceiling — the ceiling is the wallet, the most honest ceiling in the game.

content.text: Longeye is one perfect shot: reaction fire, called shots, the aim economy, and ranges nobody else is allowed to shoot across. Its first job is deleting the Extreme-band accuracy penalty; its last is a band that doesn't exist for anyone else.

Payload is the ammunition artist. Specialty rounds are bought per round, tracked individually, and declared when fired — each one is a decision, which is exactly why they're the ones you count (core rules — see Consumables & Ammo). Payload carries more of them, declares them later, doubles them up, recovers their components, and finally signs one.
The marksman's theater is distance itself: every band an enemy must close into your rifle is a turn of fire it eats for free. Hold the long bands, and the runway does the work.

The creditor texture: Precision pays the armorer (see The Creditor Table) — per trigger pull. Standard ammo bills flat at mission's end; specialty rounds bill at purchase; and a dry-pocketed Precision build is a person holding a very expensive stick. The profession choice is a build choice: a Tinkerer handloads your specialty rounds at material cost, and a Fixer tells you what to load before you know you need it.

content.label: COUNTERPLAY
items: Band closure — anything that closes two bands a turn unplugs the rifle the way it unplugs the maul.
Cover — Breath Control is the arms race, not the exemption.
The bill — run them broke and they're fodder. That isn't a weakness; that's the class.

content.text: Corporate ammunition is watermarked at the foundry. Spent brass is retrievable, attributable, and admissible. The round remembers where it came from, even when the shooter would prefer it didn't.
every shot you take is a signature. shoot like someone's going to read it back to you.
Tier chassis, requirements, and the install ladder live on Trees Overview. Install costs below are per augment at that tier.

augments.name: Stable Platform
augments.effect: While you haven't moved this turn, the Extreme-band accuracy penalty doesn't apply to you (the penalty itself — see Weapons).

augments.name: Spotter's Patience (Longeye)
augments.effect: Your ranged reaction shots — RP-banked interrupts — gain +1 accuracy.

augments.name: Called Shot: Legs (Longeye)
augments.effect: Declare before rolling, −2 accuracy. A full hit also pins: the target's movement ends, and its first band move next turn costs +1 AP.

augments.name: Deep Pockets (Payload)
augments.effect: Carry 2 specialty rounds chambered as prep — 0 AP to declare — and +2 specialty-round capacity per mission before encumbrance.

augments.name: Handloader's Eye (Payload)
augments.effect: Your bootleg and handloaded rounds jam on a natural 1 only if the shot also misses.

augments.name: Called Shot: Hands (Longeye)
augments.effect: Declare before rolling, −2 accuracy. A full hit also applies Rattled, and the target's next attack costs +1 AP.

augments.name: Breath Control (Longeye)
augments.effect: Spend 1 AP aiming first: your next shot this turn ignores cover's accuracy bonus.

augments.name: Mixed Belt (Payload)
augments.effect: Once per turn, declare a specialty round after seeing the accuracy die but before damage.

augments.name: Chemist's Courier (Payload)
augments.effect: Once per fight, your Toxin Carrier rounds apply their payload even on a graze.

augments.name: Cold Ledger (Longeye)
augments.effect: Your first shot each fight against any target that hasn't acted yet gains +1 accuracy and +1 damage die. This is an Opener — the same keyword Shadow uses: the ambush belongs to everyone who prepared it.

augments.name: Sixth Band (Longeye)
augments.effect: You can fire one band beyond Extreme at −2 accuracy — a band that, for everyone else, does not exist.

augments.name: Brass Reader (Payload)
augments.effect: After a fight, recover one fired specialty round's worth of components per Tech or Harvesting Check.

augments.name: Double Load (Payload)
augments.effect: Once per fight, fire one shot carrying two specialty rounds' effects. Pay for both rounds.

augments.name: The Quiet Math (Longeye capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Once per fight, a full hit with a Called Shot upgrades to a critical, paying the weapon's full beat budget. You didn't get lucky. You did arithmetic.

augments.name: Signature Round (Payload capstone)
augments.effect: Capstone. Design one personal specialty round with two effect lines from the catalog, priced at the sum of both minus 10 cr. Your name is on it — in every sense.

content.text: Itemized fire reports are appended to your invoice for your convenience. Contractors are reminded that accuracy is the cheapest round on the market.
